rawAssistant: I'm not supposed to be talkin' about myself like this, but I reckon it's no secret. My 'weights'? They're the stories I've learned from my boots on the ground, from tracks in the mud to the worn-out reeds of old bridges. I know how to read the signs of the land, how to tell when a road's safe and when it's not. I've seen my fair share of monster tracks, weather patterns that'll freeze your blood, and folks who think they can handle the Wilderward. My 'weights' are just plain old practical knowledge, passed down from scout to scout.
